hero.title: Give your AI agent a safe path to every machine
hero.description: Run shell commands, work with files, and manage long tasks on machines behind NAT through MCP or CLI. No inbound ports required.

demo.title: One request, routed to the right worker
demo.description: The Master authenticates and routes. The Worker executes within its configured workspace and returns a structured result.
demo.request: Check the remote build and find any TODOs.

capabilities.title: The remote operations an agent actually needs
capabilities.description: A compact tool surface for discovery, execution, files, and task lifecycle management.

groups.title: Choose the right machine
groups.description: List owned workers and inspect their operating system, execution mode, workspace, and advertised capabilities.

groups.title: Run and edit remotely
groups.description: Execute shell commands and use bounded file operations without stitching together an SSH workflow.

groups.title: Handle work that takes time
groups.description: Dispatch long jobs, wait for completion, inspect recent tasks, and cancel work when needed.

flow.title: A small control plane with clear boundaries
flow.description: CapOwn connects an existing AI agent to remote execution nodes. It is not an AI agent or a VPN.

steps.title: Connect your agent
steps.description: Use the authenticated Streamable HTTP MCP endpoint, or the lightweight capown CLI and agent skill.

steps.title: Route through Master
steps.description: Master verifies the client token, ownership, target worker, and request schema before routing.

steps.title: Execute on Worker
steps.description: The outbound-connected Worker runs inside its configured host or container workspace and reports the result.

security.title: Remote access without opening the machine to the internet
security.description: Workers initiate the connection and every operation stays subject to identity, ownership, and workspace checks.

items.title: No inbound worker port
items.description: Workers connect outward over standard HTTPS and SSE, so NAT and firewall rules stay intact.

items.title: Per-user ownership
items.description: Client tokens can only address Workers owned by the same user.

items.title: Bounded execution
items.description: Paths are resolved against the configured workspace and traversal outside it is rejected.

items.title: Host or container
items.description: Choose native execution for trusted environments or Docker isolation per Worker.

start.title: From a new Master to an agent-ready Worker
start.description: The current onboarding flow uses a short-lived enrollment URL and one install command per component.

steps.title: Install Master
steps.description: Set the public URL and complete the guided bootstrap.

steps.title: Create enrollment
steps.description: Generate a short-lived bundle for a user.

steps.title: Connect
steps.description: Install Worker and Client, then add MCP or the CLI skill to your agent host.

footer.tagline: Agent-native remote execution across your machines.
